theory.intro: Because $1\text{ L} = 1000\text{ mL}$, a capacity in millilitres can be written as a decimal number of litres.
facts: $1\text{ L} = 1000\text{ mL}$, so $1\text{ mL} = \tfrac{1}{1000}\text{ L} = 0.001\text{ L}$.
To change **mL to L**, divide by $1000$ (move the point $3$ places left).
To change **L to mL**, multiply by $1000$ (move the point $3$ places right).
$500\text{ mL} = 0.5\text{ L}$ and $250\text{ mL} = 0.25\text{ L}$ are useful to remember.
steps: Decide which way you are converting.
mL → L: divide by $1000$.  L → mL: multiply by $1000$.
Write the answer with the correct unit.
